title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bursitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bursitis are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, often taken orally or topically to alleviate bursitis symptoms. Bursitis occurs when the fluid-filled sacs (bursae) that cushion bones, tendons, and muscles near joints become inflamed. Certain herbs, such as willow bark, turmeric, and devil's claw, have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them ideal for bursitis treatment. These tinctures can reduce inflammation, manage pain, and improve joint mobility, allowing individuals to resume daily activities with greater ease and comfort. For example, a person with hip bursitis may take a willow bark tincture, experiencing less pain and increased mobility, enabling them to enjoy walks or exercise without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with bursitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the affected area. Bursitis occurs when the bursae, small fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints, become inflamed. Arnica flower tincture can help alleviate the pain and swelling associated with bursitis by stimulating blood flow and promoting healing. Additionally, arnica has been shown to have analgesic properties, which can help relieve the discomfort and tenderness caused by bursitis. By reducing inflammation and providing pain relief, arnica flower tincture can help promote mobility and speed up the healing process in individuals suffering from bursitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected bursa, thanks to its active constituents, sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin.
Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture acts as a natural pain reliever, alleviating discomfort caused by bursitis, due to the presence of volatile oils like thymol and camphor.

Circulatory stimulant: Arnica flower tincture improves blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, as a result of its active compounds, including flavonoids and tannins.

Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture's antioxidant properties help protect cells from damage, supporting tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress in bursitis, owing to its high concentration of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Immune system support: Arnica flower tincture boosts the immune system, aiding in the body's ability to fight off infection and promote healing in bursitis, attributed to its active components, such as polysaccharides and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the arnica plant are used in making medicinal tea for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant are used in making medicinal tea for bursitis as they contain compounds that help alleviate pain and promote healing of the inflamed bursa.
Rhyzomes: Arnica rhyzomes are used in medicinal tea for bursitis because they have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh arnica flowers, carefully avoiding the toxic leaves and stems, during their full bloom in late spring or early summer.
Clean the flowers gently by rinsing under cold running water, then let them air dry on a paper towel.
Place the dried arnica flowers in a clean glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters, and add 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum, ensuring the flowers are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently once a day.

Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply topically, as needed, for bursitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tincture helps with bursitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, work together to alleviate symptoms of bursitis by promoting blood flow and healing. Daisy tincture can also help to strengthen the immune system, which can speed up the recovery process and prevent future flare-ups. Additionally, it may help to improve mobility and flexibility in the affected joint, allowing for a faster return to normal activities.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected bursa, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Analgesic: The analgesic effect of daisy tincture helps alleviate bursitis-related pain by inhibiting the production of pain-inducing chemicals, due to the active constituents like taraxasterol and chamazulene.

Antioxidant: Daisy tincture's antioxidant properties support the body's response to oxidative stress and promote healing in the inflamed bursa, as a result of its high polyphenol and flavonoid content.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of daisy tincture help prevent infections in the affected bursa, as the active constituents, such as essential oils and polyphenols, inhibit bacterial growth.

Immune-modulating: Daisy tincture's ability to modulate the immune system helps regulate the body's response to inflammation, reducing the risk of chronic bursitis, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and polyacetylenes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, daisy flowers help alleviate bursitis symptoms.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, daisy leaves aid in reducing inflammation and soothing bursitis pain.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory properties, daisy buds support healing and pain relief in bursitis.
Stems: Containing essential oils, daisy stems help reduce inflammation and promote recovery from bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh daisy flowers and leaves, or 1 cup of dried daisy herb, and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the daisy herb, ensuring that all plant material is fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the recommended steeping time, strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place; the tincture will last for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with bursitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the affected area. Bursitis occurs when the bursae, the small fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints, become inflamed. Pot marigold, also known as Calendula officinalis, contains flavonoids that strengthen the walls of blood vessels and reduce inflammation. By reducing inflammation, pot marigold tincture can alleviate pain and promote healing in individuals with bursitis. Additionally, pot marigold has antimicrobial properties that can prevent infection in the affected area, which is crucial for individuals with bursitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the affected bursa, providing relief from bursitis pain. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Analgesic: The tincture's pain-relieving properties help alleviate the discomfort and tenderness associated with bursitis. This is attributed to the triterpene esters and flavonoids found in pot marigold.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections in the inflamed bursa, promoting faster healing. This is due to the presence of the antimicrobial compound, thiophene.

Circulatory stimulant: The tincture's ability to improve blood flow helps accelerate the healing process in the affected bursa. This is attributed to the saponins and essential oils present in pot marigold.

Immune system support: Pot marigold tincture supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infection and promote overall healing. This is due to the presence of polysaccharides, which have immunostimulant properties.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief for bursitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for use in medicinal teas to alleviate bursitis symptoms.
Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of the pot marigold plant still contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can be beneficial for bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar halfway.
Submerge flowers with 80-proof vodka, covering them by 1-2 inches, and sealing the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, gently shaking it every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent flowers.

Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper, keeping it in a cool, dark location for up to a year. For bursitis, take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) three times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tincture helps with bursitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as capsaicin, increase blood flow and help remove excess fluid from the bursa sac, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. Additionally, bell pepper tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect cells from damage caused by inflammation, further supporting the healing process. Overall, bell pepper tincture can provide natural relief for bursitis symptoms, promoting mobility and improving quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tincture can reduce inflammation in the bursae, providing pain relief, due to the presence of flavonoids such as quercetin and luteolin.
Analgesic: It can alleviate pain associated with bursitis by stimulating the production of pain-relieving chemicals in the body, thanks to the capsaicin content.

Antioxidant: Bell pepper tincture helps protect the body from oxidative stress and supports the immune system, which can aid in the healing process of bursitis, because of its rich vitamin C and A content.
Circulation improvement: It can enhance blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, due to the presence of niacin and other B-vitamins.
Diuretic: Bell pepper tincture can help eliminate excess fluids from the body, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the bursae, because of its potassium content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating bursitis.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce swelling and pain in bursitis.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and nutrients, they provide relief from pain and inflammation in bursitis.
Stems: Contain bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of bursitis, such as pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Chop two bell peppers and one cup of anti-inflammatory herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and licorice root.
Combine herbs and peppers with 2 cups of vodka or rum in a clean jar, ensuring the mixture is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid and discarding solids.
Store tincture in a tightly sealed glass container for up to six months, taking 1-2 mL up to three times daily for bursitis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with bursitis because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. This herb is also known for its ability to improve circulation, which can help increase the delivery of nutrients and oxygen to the injured bursa, promoting healing and reducing swelling. Additionally, gotu kola has been shown to strengthen connective tissues, which can help prevent further damage to the bursa and improve overall joint health. Its potential to stimulate collagen production may also aid in the repair of damaged tissues, further contributing to the alleviation of bursitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected bursa, containing active constituents like asiaticoside and madecassoside.
Wound healing: Its wound healing properties promote tissue repair and regeneration in the inflamed bursa, primarily due to triterpene saponins like asiatic acid and madecassic acid.
Analgesic: Gotu kola tincture's analgesic effects alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bursitis, attributed to its triterpene and flavonoid content.
Improves circulation: Enhanced circulation supports the removal of excess fluids and waste products around the inflamed bursa, thanks to gotu kola's active compounds like brahmoside and brahminoside.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of gotu kola tincture help protect cells from damage and support overall joint health, which is crucial for managing bursitis, attributed to its high flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tea for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Gotu kola stems contain compounds that help reduce pain and swelling in the joints, making them useful for bursitis tea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of gotu kola have been shown to improve circulation and promote tissue healing, making them a valuable addition to bursitis tea.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola leaves and add them to a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, away from light.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with bursitis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that can reduce inflammation in the affected area. Bursitis is a condition characterized by inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs called bursae that cushion the joints. Curcumin works by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby alleviating pain and swelling associated with bursitis. Additionally, turmeric has antioxidant properties that can help repair damaged tissue and promote healing. Regular use of turmeric tincture can provide relief from bursitis symptoms and improve joint mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, mainly due to curcumin, helps reduce swelling and pain in bursitis.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic property of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcuminoids, alleviates pain caused by bursitis.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from curcumin, helps protect cells from damage and supports tissue repair in bursitis.
Improves joint mobility: Turmeric tincture's ability to enhance joint mobility, thanks to curcumin, benefits individuals with bursitis by reducing stiffness and improving range of motion.

Immune system support: Turmeric tincture's immune-boosting property, attributed to curcumin and other active compounds, helps strengthen the body's defense mechanisms, which may help manage bursitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's rhizomes are rich in curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bursitis symptoms.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves contain beneficial polyphenols that can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with bursitis.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers have antioxidant properties that can help protect cells from damage and reduce inflammation in bursitis.
Seeds: Turmeric seeds contain essential oils and curcumin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help manage bursitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth into a clean jar, discarding the solid turmeric pieces.
Label the jar with the contents and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for bursitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with bursitis because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as echinacea and cichoric acid. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and promote tissue repair in the affected area. Bursitis is often caused by overuse or injury, which can lead to inflammation and swelling in the bursae, small fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, the herbal tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of bursitis and support overall joint health. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of eastern purple coneflower may help prevent infection in the affected area, further supporting the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for bursitis as it is an inflammatory condition that affects the bursae, small fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints. The active constituents responsible for this property include chlorogenic acid, caffeic acid, and luteolin.

Analgesic: The tincture helps alleviate pain, which is crucial for bursitis as it often causes significant joint pain and tenderness. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is sesquiterpene lactones, particularly helenalin and its derivatives.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage, which is important for bursitis as inflammation can lead to oxidative stress and further tissue damage. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Immunomodulatory: The tincture helps regulate the immune system, which is important for bursitis as it is often caused by an overactive immune response. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides and alkamides.

Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower tincture has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infections, which is important for bursitis as the inflammation and swelling can make the affected area more susceptible to infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils, sesquiterpene lactones, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help alleviate bursitis-related inflammation.
Leaves: Packed with antioxidants, the leaves aid in soothing irritated bursae and promoting healing.
Stems: Containing essential nutrients, the stems support the overall health of the affected area, enhancing the tea's therapeutic effects.
Seeds: Seeds provide vital minerals and oils that help reduce pain and swelling associated with bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves at peak bloom, ensuring they are fully open and dry.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried herb into small pieces, then place in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material. Store the finished tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, away from light and heat. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for bursitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with bursitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from its unique blueish-green leaves. These properties work to reduce inflammation in the affected bursa, thereby alleviating pain and promoting healing. The tincture's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, providing relief from bursitis symptoms. Furthermore, the herbal extract's soothing qualities can help decrease stiffness and improve joint mobility, allowing for increased range of motion and overall functionality.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the affected bursa, thanks to the presence of compounds like alpha-pinene and limonene.
Analgesic: The tincture can alleviate bursitis-related pain due to its analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of eucalyptol and eudesmol.
Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections in the inflamed bursa, as it contains compounds like globulol and viridiflorol.
Immune-boosting: The tincture supports the immune system, enabling the body to better manage bursitis symptoms, due to its abundance in compounds like alpha-terpineol and aromadendrene.

Circulatory system support: Tasmanian blue gum tincture promotes blood flow and reduces inflammation in the affected area, aiding in the healing process, as it contains compounds like alpha-humulene and beta-caryophyllene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tincture plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing bursitis.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum tincture plant contains tannins that have astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the joints affected by bursitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum tincture plant have antiseptic and analgesic properties, making them useful for relieving pain and preventing infection in bursitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum tincture plant contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them helpful in reducing swelling and pain in bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest leaves from a Tasmanian blue gum tree, selecting only healthy, mature leaves; rinse thoroughly to remove dirt and bugs.
Chop or grind 2 cups of fresh leaves (or 1 cup of dried leaves) into small pieces, then place in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof or higher vodka over the leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged; seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks.
Shake the jar gently every few days to help the extraction process; after the desired steeping time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, amber glass bottle with a dropper; for bursitis, take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) up to three times daily, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tincture helps with bursitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Bursitis is a condition characterized by inflammation of the bursae, small fluid-filled sacs that cushion the joints. The herb's active compounds, particularly harpagoside, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective natural remedy for bursitis symptoms. Additionally, devil's claw may help decrease stiffness and improve mobility in the affected joints, promoting faster recovery and overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for bursitis as it is an inflammation of the bursa, a small fluid-filled sac that cushions the joints. The anti-inflammatory properties come from iridoid glycosides, such as harpagoside, present in the devil's claw herb.

Analgesic: Devil's claw tincture can alleviate pain, which is crucial for bursitis as it often causes significant joint pain. The analgesic effects are attributed to the harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Improves mobility: Devil's claw tincture can enhance joint mobility, which is important for bursitis as it can restrict movement due to pain and inflammation. The improved mobility is linked to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of devil's claw.

Antioxidant: Devil's claw tincture has antioxidant properties, which are significant for bursitis as oxidative stress can exacerbate inflammation and joint damage. The antioxidant effects are due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other phytochemicals in devil's claw.

Immune system support: Devil's claw tincture can help modulate the immune response, which is relevant for bursitis as it involves an overactive immune response leading to inflammation. The immune system support is linked to the herb's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and other immune signaling molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant contain harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in bursitis.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the devil's claw plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have a positive effect on joint health and can help alleviate symptoms of bursitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the devil's claw plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in bursitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the devil's claw plant contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect on the body and can help reduce inflammation in bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried devil's claw root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out all the liquid from the root.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store in a cool, dark place. Recommended dosage is 1-2 mL, 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with bursitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Bursitis is characterized by inflammation and pain in the bursae, small fluid-filled sacs that cushion joints. The active compounds in St. John's wort, particularly hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective natural remedy for bursitis. By promoting tissue healing and reducing inflammation, St. John's wort tincture can help manage the symptoms of bursitis and support overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's Wort tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in the affected bursa, thanks to the active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic: Its pain-relieving property is crucial for managing bursitis symptoms, as hypericin and hyperforin interact with nerve cell receptors to reduce pain perception.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property helps prevent infections in the inflamed bursa, as hyperforin exhibits antibacterial and antiviral properties.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property supports healing by reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair, with active constituents such as hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Immunomodulatory: St. John's Wort tincture's ability to regulate the immune response is essential for managing bursitis, as it helps control inflammation and promotes overall immune system balance, attributable to active components like hypericin, hyperforin, and hypericum extracts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they're fully open and dry, typically in mid-summer.
Chop or crush 2 ounces of the herb, placing it in a clean, dry glass jar, then fill the jar with 1 pint of vodka or rum (at least 80 proof).
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years. Take 30 drops, 3 times a day, as needed for bursitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are commonly used in making medicinal tea for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of St. John's Wort are used in tea preparation for bursitis as they contain hypericin, which has pain-relieving and antibacterial effects.
Stems: Although less potent than the flowers and leaves, the stems of St. John's Wort can still contribute to the medicinal tea for bursitis, providing additional anti-inflammatory benefits.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bursitis with herbal tinctures is by using anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs. For instance, a tincture made from turmeric and ginger roots can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Willow bark and Jamaican dogwood tinctures can also provide significant pain relief. To maximize the benefits, take the tinctures orally, three times a day, and always follow the recommended dosage. Remember, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bursitis that are treated with herbal tinctures include tendonitis, arthritis, and muscle strains. These conditions often cause pain and inflammation in the joints and surrounding tissues, similar to bursitis. Herbal tinctures can provide relief by reducing inflammation and alleviating pain. For example, willow bark and turmeric tinctures are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, while St. John's Wort and California poppy tinctures can help alleviate muscle and nerve pain. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment, including herbal tinctures, to ensure safety and effectiveness.
